doc:
ssh-add -l                  — list loaded keys
ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519   — add a key
ssh-add -D                  — drop all keys
ssh-add -t 8h <key>         — load with 8h lifetime

ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "you@example.com"
ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C "..."
ssh-keygen -y -f <privkey>  — extract public key
ssh-keygen -lf <pubkey>     — fingerprint

desc: Copy your public key into a remote authorized_keys
Set up SSH access on a vanilla host (key + sshd hardening)
doc:
bootstrap-remote <host>            — dry-run
bootstrap-remote <host> --apply    — execute
bootstrap-remote <host> --apply --skip-harden  — key only

desc: Generate + upload install script for a remote host
doc:
bootstrap-dotfiles <host>             — SSH path
bootstrap-dotfiles --from-jot <pfx>   — self-bootstrap, pull system files from S3
